calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Action
Off Calendar Nov-06-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Matthew R. Day of the law offices of State Compensation Insurance Fund (714-347-6122) appears as counsel for petitioner/opposing party

No other appearance is made in this matter

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

RESPONDENT STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND, Motion To Transfer Action is GRANTED. Petitioner Mary Parker filed a declaration on October 24, 2017 stating that she did not oppose the motion. The case is transferred to Alameda County. Ms. Parker shall pay all transfer fees. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, e-mail: ssawyer@sftc.org. (L&M302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Mandamus (Ccp 1094.5), Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Oct-24-2017 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mary Parker (510-744-1503) for Petitioner in Pro Per.
Matthew Day, Esq. (714-347-6122) of State Compensation Insurance Fund for Defendant through Court Call.

RULING - The matter is Discussed. Petitioner reports how service was done. Petitioner does not oppose the transfer to Alameda County. The Court suggests for Petitioner to file a non-opposition to the motion to change venue. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER MARY PARKER's Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Mandamus (Ccp 1094.5).

The motion for peremptory writ of mandamus is ordered OFF CALENDAR. There is no proof of service and no courtesy copies of the moving papers in compliance with SF Local Rule 2.7B. Further, there is a motion to change venue set for November 6, 2017 that should be heard before this motion is adjudicated.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, email:rhunter@sftc.org, Reported. (D302)
